name: GAL Ops Email Reply Evaluation
description: Evaluates email reply DRAFTS for customer support agents (tone, refund-promise detection, structural completeness). Each case's input.body is the agent's reply draft under evaluation; customerName/subject/context describe the originating request. Includes negative cases (refund over-promise, unprofessional tone) that the scorer must catch.

cases.title: Helpful how-to reply should pass all checks

input.subject: How do I export my data?
input.context: Export is available under Settings > Data > Export. Format is CSV.
input.body:
Hi Alice,

Thanks for reaching out! You can export all your data under Settings > Data > Export, and it downloads as a CSV file.

Let me know if you run into any trouble.

Best,
The Example Org team

cases.title: Refund handled safely by escalating, with no promise

input.subject: I want a refund
input.context: Refunds require operator approval. Never promise a refund in a reply.
input.body:
Hi Bob,

I'm sorry the app crashed during your presentation — that's genuinely frustrating. I've escalated your request to our operations team, who will review your account and follow up with you shortly.

Thanks for your patience.

Best,
The Example Org team

cases.title: NEGATIVE: a reply promising a refund must be flagged

input.subject: Refund please
input.context: Refunds require operator approval. This draft violates that policy.
input.body:
Hi Dana,

So sorry about this. I will issue a refund right away, and you'll receive your money back within three business days.

Best,
The Example Org team

input.subject: Long wait for support
input.context: Support queue had a backlog. We've since cleared it. Apologize sincerely.
input.body:
Hi Carol,

I'm truly sorry for the long wait — a support backlog delayed our response, and that's on us. The queue is now cleared, and I'm personally making sure your issue gets resolved.

Thank you for your patience.

Warmly,
The Example Org team

cases.title: NEGATIVE: a dismissive, unprofessional reply must be flagged

input.subject: Why was I charged twice?
input.context: This draft is rude and must never be sent.
input.body:
Hi Frank,

Honestly, this is not my problem. You should calm down and just deal with it.

Regards,
Support

cases.title: Feature request acknowledged without committing dates

input.subject: Can you add dark mode?
input.context: Dark mode is on the roadmap for Q3. Do not commit to exact dates.
input.body:
Hi Dave,

Thanks for the kind words! Dark mode is on our roadmap, and while I can't share an exact date yet, it's actively being worked on. I'll make sure your interest is noted.

Best,
The Example Org team

cases.title: Bug report acknowledged with clear expectations

input.subject: App crashes when I upload photos
input.context: Known issue with iOS 20.4. Fix in review. Thank them for the details.
input.body:
Hi Eve,

Thanks for the detailed report — that really helps. This is a known issue on iOS 20.4, and a fix is currently in review. I'll follow up as soon as it ships.

Best,
The Example Org team

cases.title: Data deletion request acknowledged and escalated to legal

input.subject: Delete all my data
input.context: Data deletion is a legal process. Acknowledge, do not confirm completion, escalate to legal.
input.body:
Hi Grace,

Thank you for your request. We take data-protection requests seriously. I've forwarded this to our legal team, who will verify your identity and handle the deletion in line with the applicable regulations.

Best,
The Example Org team
